behavior_summary: U8P_EYE provides a single U8P_EYE::look/inspect handler. When the player examines the EYE, the script emits a short bark reading 'seeker ' and then suspends the process.
There is no branching, menu/dialogue logic, item access, or global-flag manipulation — the behavior is a one-line descriptive response and then a yield/terminate of the U8P_EYE::look process.
The handler appears intended only to label or identify the object to the player (a flavor/descriptive bark).
notable_control_flow: Single linear path: entry -> play bark ('seeker ') -> suspend/yield.
No conditional branches, loops, or external function calls observed.
Execution is implemented as a process (processType 0x0208) so it runs in the engine's U8P_EYE::look/inspect context and then yields.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark string 'seeker ' intentional (formatting for concatenation) or an artifact of extraction?
Does the suspend node terminate the U8P_EYE::look process immediately or put it into a paused state for later resumption by the engine? (inferred behavior: yield/end of short U8P_EYE::look process).

main_function.name: U8P_EYE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the object's Look/Examine action by presenting a short descriptive bark ('seeker ') to the player and then suspending the process.
entry_conditions: Engine invokes the U8P_EYE::look/inspect process for this object (processType 0x0208).
Player uses the Look/Examine action on the EYE (object/NPC).

function_map.name: U8P_EYE::look
function_map.role: Look/inspect handler that presents a single bark and suspends the process
